title: CORDYCEPIN
description: Cordycepin (3'-deoxyadenosine) is a natural nucleoside analog derived from Cordyceps militaris fungus that structurally resembles adenosine. It works by modulating multiple cellular pathways including adenosine receptor signaling, oxidative stress response (Keap1/Nrf2/HO-1), and inflammatory cascades, demonstrating anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, neuroprotective, and metabolic regulatory effects.

research.title: Cordycepin combined with antioxidant effects improves fatigue caused by excessive exercise
research.summary: Cordycepin enhanced exercise endurance, increased liver and muscle glycogen content, decreased fatigue metabolites (lactic acid, creatine kinase), and improved learning/memory deficits from over-exercise by modulating the Keap1/Nrf2/HO-1 signaling pathway and BDNF expression.

research.title: Anti-inflammatory effects of cordycepin: A review
research.summary: Comprehensive review demonstrating cordycepin's anti-inflammatory properties through multiple mechanisms including modulation of NF-κB, MAPK pathways, and cytokine production, with therapeutic potential for inflammatory diseases.

research.title: Antidepressant-like effects of cordycepin in a mice model of chronic unpredictable mild stress
research.summary: Cordycepin demonstrated antidepressant-like effects in stress-induced animal models, suggesting potential mood-regulating and stress-response modulating properties.

research.title: Structural and pharmacological insights into cordycepin for neoplasms and metabolic disorders
research.summary: Review highlighting cordycepin's significant therapeutic potential in alleviating hyperlipidemia, regulating glucose metabolism, and demonstrating anti-tumor effects through multiple mechanisms including apoptosis induction and immune modulation.

sentiment.summary: User experiences with cordycepin are limited in the available data, with research showing promising anti-fatigue, anti-inflammatory, and metabolic benefits, but insufficient Reddit user feedback to establish strong consensus on subjective effects, tolerability, or practical efficacy.

effects.label: Exercise Performance:
effects.description: Demonstrated significant improvements in exercise endurance, glycogen restoration, and reduction of fatigue biomarkers in animal studies

effects.label: Metabolic Regulation:
effects.description: Shows therapeutic potential for hyperlipidemia and glucose metabolism disorders through multiple molecular mechanisms

effects.label: Stress Response:
effects.description: Exhibits antidepressant-like effects in chronic stress models, suggesting mood-regulating properties

effects.label: Bioavailability Concerns:
effects.description: Research indicates strategies to enhance bioavailability may be necessary for optimal therapeutic effects

dosage.label: Research Doses:
dosage.description: Animal studies typically use doses scaled appropriately to body weight, with human equivalent doses requiring careful conversion

dosage.label: Timing Considerations:
dosage.description: Anti-fatigue effects suggest potential benefits when taken before exercise or during periods of high physical demand

dosage.label: Combination Approaches:
dosage.description: Often studied in combination with other Cordyceps compounds or antioxidants for enhanced effects

dosage.label: Duration:
dosage.description: Effects on fatigue, metabolism, and inflammation may require sustained supplementation for optimal benefits

sideEffects.label: Limited Human Data:
sideEffects.description: Most safety data comes from animal studies; comprehensive human side effect profiles are not well-established

sideEffects.label: Adenosine-Related Effects:
sideEffects.description: As an adenosine analog, potential effects on cardiovascular and nervous system should be considered

sideEffects.label: Metabolic Impact:
sideEffects.description: Blood sugar and lipid-regulating effects require monitoring in those with metabolic conditions

sideEffects.label: General Tolerance:
sideEffects.description: Research suggests good tolerance in animal models, but long-term human safety data is limited

availability.label: Natural Source:
availability.description: Derived from Cordyceps militaris fungus, available through various supplement manufacturers

availability.label: Quality Variability:
availability.description: Cordycepin content can vary significantly between products; standardized extracts are preferable for consistent dosing

availability.label: Regulatory Status:
availability.description: Generally available as a dietary supplement, though quality control and purity standards vary by manufacturer
